title: Was the 14 year timeskip really necessary Split
display_title: Was the 14 year timeskip really necessary Split
wikitext:
[]: Was the 14-year timeskip *really* necessary? [Split]             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Fri Jun 28, 2013 10:23 pm [Split from just after this post . - Monk] Couldn't it just be everyone hating Shinji for causing Third Impact/Near-Third Impact but in present day? Like in the original series, he was trapped in Eva-01, but only for like a month or so. 3.0 could have potentially him coming out after the same amount of time, but to everyone's immediate hatred. This raises the question AGAIN about the 14 year time skip though, and if it was necessary for this.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Jun 29, 2013 2:18 am It was necessary if the intention was to use it as an excuse to create a new blank slate of possibilities for the direction of the narrative. Anno's intention clearly isn't to simply redo the series; he really wants to take it beyond where he'd ever gone with the original. A timeskip is a good excuse to throw in some radial changes to the formula and see where it takes the story.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jun 29, 2013 2:13 pm Reasons beyond emphasized alienation of the main character and other narrative tones and aesthetics as to why the time skip was necessary? Are you looking for reason within the story as to why thids was necessary? I guess it'd have to do with why it was Shinji had to be launched into space with Unit 01 to begin with, and I'm afraid 3.0 doesn't provide those reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by TruthfulLie » Sat Jun 29, 2013 2:24 pm Even that question seems to irrelevant at this point since virtually nothing is known of the missing fourteen years.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Sat Jun 29, 2013 7:04 pm I'm thinking it has to do with impact mechanics, after 2nd impact 14 years (or 15?) passed for the angels to start showing up, after n3I and the subsequent actual 3I also 14 had to pass... I guess is all connected and they are trying to showcase that the 14 years isn't just a random number or a cheap narrative device to give humanity time to prepare for the angels attacks, but a mechanism of the impacts themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by peripateia » Sat Jun 29, 2013 9:02 pm I'd say that 'necessary' or 'unnecessary' aren't the best words to describe the time skip as a plot device/choice. Sachi and FFF4E already said it, but the time skip allowed 3.0 to move beyond the original eva 'canon', hence "You cannot Redo" (or can you?). I think this is Anno's eccentricity at his finest! He loves to bait and disorient his audience, and 1.0/2.0, being rather straightforward narratives, didn't give him many chances to do that. The time skip is an information blackout that separates Shinji (and the audience) from familiar surroundings/personalities. The question, for me, is no longer "Was this skip necessary?" but "how will Shinji bridge this 14 yr gap?" and "how will this disorientation affect Shinji (and the audience) 's relationship with Shinji's past friends, peers and associates?" Unfortunately, much as I'd like to fantasize about changing Rebuild to suit my inner fanboy (there's always fanfiction!) I have to accept grudgingly that all I can do as a viewer is wait for Khara to spoon feed me...I'm still hungry for eva after NGE and EoE, I'll swallow anything they put in front of me at this point

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Jun 29, 2013 9:20 pm Tangent about Shinji's space vacation and who launched him up there went here .

[]:             Quote Post by wiser3754 » Thu Oct 03, 2013 7:04 am A view I find on the 14 year time skip was to effectively showcase Shinji as 14 year old baby essentially. As a baby you come into the world born ignorant of it and how it works until you slowly grow to adapt to it. While Shinji may be 14 years old, he's only been in this world for less than a week, making him essentially a baby in comparison, ignorant, terrified and unsure of how this world works. The only reason I making my view on this topic is of course the name calling by Asuka and Mari towards the end of the film. It's not insulting, it's true, he's just a baby and it's best he goes to see the world a little. Could be his second turn at not shutting himself out from the world or others after his abandonment issues and death of his mother left him as a shut out till his father came calling. Add, destroy or ignore, I was just scouring through the posts and thought I'd bring fresh perspective to the topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Oct 03, 2013 7:28 am Looking in this context... Calling him a baby IS insulting, or is aimed to be an insult. He is blamed for being a newborn baby. Actually it all started when he was unexpectdly found in Eva, so it could actualy go into "why were you even born?" territory, with his environment being disappointed with appearance of unwanted child. Which is again, pretty harsh since symbolically it makes Shinji an unwanted child, blamed for being born and bringing problems with the fact he was being born.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 9:12 am @ wiser3754 : It's also worth noting that he spent 14 years absorbed inside EVA-01, the same number of years he lived in a corporal form, as if it served to metaphorically "erase" his previous life and make it start anew with a clean slate, a sort of second chance at life of not shutting himself out and not being manipulated by his father(who could have partially orchestrated his son' s shut-in character by sending him living alone with his teacher)... The problem I have with that interpretation is that so far it contradicts his situation : he doesn't have a clean slate, he start with a very negative slate that makes him treated as a war criminal(which darkens even more by the time of the end of the movie!), which would incite him to shut himself out even more(remember his comment to Kaworu : "You say that, but my father, Evas, Misato-san... I can't believe in anyone, or anything!") and on top of it he falls again under Gendo's manipulations mere hours after his "rebirth"! But it could be true for after Q : now apparently Gendo really don't have any more use for Shinji and abandoned him at WILLE's hands, and after everything he has done to him and the rest of the world, Shinji definitively don't want anything more to do with him, so for the first time since Yui's disappearance(or maybe even his birth !) Shinji is truly free from his father's schemes. Now let's see in FINAL if he manages to break of his tendency of shutting himself out from the world or others... that's it if he's given a chance at it and WILLE don't close themselves completely to him.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Talos » Thu Oct 03, 2013 1:21 pm ^Shinji may be free from being a direct pawn in his fathers schemes but not the consequences of those schemes which he, Wille and the rest of the world will feel throughout Final. In addition I imagine he will be either directly or indirectly part of Wille's attempts to thwart Gendo and thus still trapped in the shadow of the works of his father. If he and the wider world is to be completely free of Gendo's influence I still think there's a ways to go and given the amount of devastation wrought by Seele and Gendo their shadows will probably hang over the world for pretty much forever. As for the necessity of the time skip I suppose Shinji's and the viewer's alienation could have been achieved without it but then Q would be missing what really sets it apart and what I consider the most interesting decision taken in it's development.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 2:11 pm ^ When I said he was free, it was in the sense that from the first time of his life he may be really completely free of his choices, which was never the case before in his whole life : - when he first arrived Gendo told him that if he wasn't gonna to pilot to fight Sachiel, then he must leave, it seems like Shinji had a choice... but Gendo brought an half-dead Rei just after, who obviously couldn't pilot good enough to defeat Sachiel. So basically Shinji's choice was "leave and have the blood of this girl in your hands, making you a scumbag, and probably lead to mankind's extinction" or "get in the fucking robot Shinji". Morally, Shinji just can't leave Rei at her fate, he may be passive, cynical at times and sometimes very me-centric, but he's not the kind of asshole who would let an innocent girl go to her death just to make a point with his father. And Gendo knew that, that's why he smirked when Shinji finally accepted, because, as always "all according to the plan". - he was told by Misato that he could resign as a pilot anytime, but that was false : Gendo needs him for his plan of awakening EVA-01 alongside Rei, so if Shinji really resign, his father will make him kidnapped and brainwashed, Fuyutsuki even discussed this possibility with Gendo should Shinji refuses to participate in operation Yashima. - when the Dummy System was completed, he resigned, and this time Gendo let him, probably because he though that the DS would be enough to awake EVA-01... but in the end Yui rejected it, so had Shinji not came back everyone would had died(or at least Misato and Co), so in the end there wasn't any choice... that's it if Gendo didn't still planned to use Shinji later to awake EVA-01 and planned to make him kidnapped, brainwashed and kept in an hidden location for the moment he planned to awake the Eva!(remember that Msiato said that even after resigning Shinji would still be under surveillance and his freedom of movement limited) - when Mark.09 appeared in Q, Shinji followed her by his own volition... but had he refused, I really doubt that Rei-Q would had called it a day and left empty-handed, she would had taken him by force. And once in neo-NERV even if Shinji refuses to pilot again even with Kaworu's pleas, Gendo would had probably brainwashed him again, or threatened Rei-Q if Shinji didn't discovered the truth about her yet. When you look at it, the only choices Shinji actually had was to do something willingly or not, even if most of the time he didn't even know that. He was never once truly free from his father, but now at the end of Q it's different : Gendo don't have any use for him anymore(I hope), and WILLE obviously lost all faith in him so they probably won't push him to do anything to help the fight, so if Shinji did join the fight, this time it will be by his own volition, without any hidden force at play that manipulated him and the events so he actually didn't really had a choice. As for the consequences of what Gendo and SEELE did, well sometimes shit happens and you have to deal with it, that's how life is. Learning to accept that sometime bad things happens is part of becoming an adult, none of us chose to live through that big economical crisis, yet we are all living its consequences... which doesn't mean we must just take everything life throw at us and don't try to change things of course, that's exactly what WILLE is trying to do! Shinji and the rest of the world were already living through the consequences of his father and SEELE's actions since they're the ones behind 2I, so it's not like the situation is new...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Oct 03, 2013 2:50 pm 14 years was necessary if only for the sake of seeing a more no-nonsense-taking Maya. The 14 year gap wasn't nonsensical in its placement in the story in the sense that Anno could have accomplished the same effect with a billion other methods, so I tend not to question it.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 2:57 pm Sachi wrote: It was necessary if the intention was to use it as an excuse to create a new blank slate of possibilities for the direction of the narrative. Anno's intention clearly isn't to simply redo the series; he really wants to take it beyond where he'd ever gone with the original.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 4:54 pm Well, at least the "Impact involving Lilith that brought global devastation" of EoE fits what apparently happened during the time skip. The problem is that we can't just "skip it" as something unimportant, since apparently Shinji and/or EVA-01 got somehow implicated in it and is the root of WILLE's treatment of him, which led to his escape to neo-NERV and finally his obsessive desire to atone for what he supposedly did. So sooner or later we'll have to delves into these events, if only to determine Shinji's exact responsibility.

[]:             Quote Post by Charsi » Thu Oct 03, 2013 6:41 pm Well if you remember EoE, Shinji was unresponsive while Asuka berated him for not coming to help her against the MP series (sound familiar?). Then, after Asuka is defeated, Eva-01 activates itself, grabs Shinji and it plus the Eva series begin the impact. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Thu Oct 03, 2013 8:47 pm TruthfulLie wrote: Even that question seems to irrelevant at this point since virtually nothing is known of the missing fourteen years.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 04, 2013 3:18 am Charsi wrote: Well if you remember EoE, Shinji was unresponsive while Asuka berated him for not coming to help her against the MP series (sound familiar?). Then, after Asuka is defeated, Eva-01 activates itself, grabs Shinji and it plus the Eva series begin the impact. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 04, 2013 4:34 pm ElMariachi wrote: Or maybe N3I was a "first step" in a chain of events that led to A3I, and thus the responsibility is indirectly traced back at him?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Oct 04, 2013 4:52 pm ^ Well, that's the only explanation I could imagine to justify the intense level of hostility and grudge(if not outright hate) almost everyone in WILLE is feeling toward him, or they are the biggest dicks in anime history.

Like in the original series, he was trapped in Eva-01, but only for like a month or so. 3.0 could have potentially him coming out after the same amount of time
